Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue To Yolo County Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 397 Subdivision (C) By Defendants Esteban Ortiz And Brandon Binford
Off Calendar Sep-09-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ESTEBAN ORTIZ and BRANDON BINFORD'S Motion To Transfer Venue To Yolo County Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 397 Subdivision (C):

Defendants Ortiz and Binford's motion to change venue to Yolo County based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice per Code of Civ. Proc. sec. 397 is denied.

Defendants have the burden to establish that the inconvenience of the witnesses warrants changing venue. (See Rothschild v. Superior Court (1963) 216 Cal.App.2d 778, 779.) In deciding this motion, the Court generally does not consider the convenience of the defendant party witnesses but may consider the convenience of Dignity's employees if it is likely that plaintiff will call them to testify. (See Baird v. Smith (1937) 21 Cal.App.2d 221, 224-225; J.C. Millet Co. v. Latchford-Marble Glass Co. (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 218, 227.) Moreover, the Court may rely on reasonable inferences from the declarations. (See Pearson v. Superior Court (1962) 199 Cal.App.2d 69, 79 ["The trial court may rely not only on the direct facts set forth in the affidavits, but also on any reasonable and relevant inference arising therefrom."].) Defendant's reliance on Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, which involves a forum non conveniens motion, is badly misplaced.

Defendants' declarations are insufficient to warrant granting the motion. The affidavits supporting such a motion "must set forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each, and the reasons why the attendance of each would be inconvenient." (Peiser v. Mettler (1958) 50 Cal.2d 594, 607.)

In this case, defendants list several witnesses who appear to be Dignity employees because they worked at the hospital. Some of the cited witnesses' last names are not provided and several of them are "unknown." There is no showing that plaintiff is likely to call these persons as witnesses or the nature of their specific testimony. In identical language, the declarants state "[u]pon information and belief" that the witnesses reside/work in Yolo County and speculate that travelling to San Francisco will be inconvenient. (Ortiz Decl., par. 4; Binford Decl., par. 4; Ninke Decl., par. 7.) (See Baird, 21 Cal.App.2d at 224 ["Affidavits for a change of venue which are based only on information and belief are of little to no value"].) The motion is denied because defendants failed to maintain their evidentiary burden.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
